opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: CLAUDE.md is confirmed placeholder (14 bytes). Project complexity (10 packages, on-chain validators, formal spec) makes this high-value.

opportunity_map.title: Create .aiignore trust boundaries for Hydra on-chain validators (hydra-plutus/) and cryptographic operations
opportunity_map.evidence: .aiignore ABSENT. Security-critical paths: hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/Head.hs (head validator), hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/Deposit.hs (deposit validator), hydra-plutus/validators/deposit.ak (Aiken validator), hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/Crypto.hs, hydra-plutus/scripts/vHead.plutus, hydra-plutus/scripts/mHead.plutus
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Hydra handles real funds on-chain. PR #2536 refactored validators. Trust boundaries essential before AI adoption.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to expand mutation tests for vHead and vDeposit validators after Hydra V2 init-phase removal
opportunity_map.evidence: hydra-tx/testlib/Test/Hydra/Tx/Mutation.hs (mutation framework), hydra-tx/test/Hydra/Tx/Contract/Close/ (existing contract tests), PR #2536 (b7ad7a8) removed vInitial and vCommit validators leaving vHead and vDeposit as sole on-chain attack surface
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Novel opportunity specific to post-V2 refactor. Mutation framework exists. Remaining validators are the entire attack surface.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to generate Haddock documentation for hydra-tx transaction types and hydra-node HeadLogic modules
opportunity_map.evidence: hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/*.hs (15+ modules: Close.hs, Contest.hs, Deposit.hs, Fanout.hs, Init.hs, Increment.hs, etc.), hydra-node/src/Hydra/HeadLogic/*.hs (State.hs, Input.hs, Outcome.hs, StateEvent.hs), hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/*.hs (12 modules). publish-docs.yaml workflow exists.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Targets specific packages and module paths. Haddock coverage gap is MEDIUM confidence (cannot read source).

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to trace HeadLogic state transitions when debugging snapshot liveness and deposit coordination bugs
opportunity_map.evidence: hydra-node/src/Hydra/HeadLogic.hs + HeadLogic/State.hs, Input.hs, Outcome.hs (state machine). PR #2560 (v0d1ch): 3 snapshot liveness bugs — version race in RequestedSnapshot, deposit dropped mid-snapshot via selectNextDeposit, deposits from other heads via unfiltered pendingDeposits. External formal spec at cardano-scaling/hydra-formal-specification.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Specific to HeadLogic state machine. Cites 3 concrete recent bugs with root causes.

opportunity_map.title: Use AI to generate structured PR descriptions beyond checklist template for Hydra protocol changes
opportunity_map.evidence: .github/pull_request_template.md (270 bytes, checklist: CHANGELOG/Documentation/Haddocks/TODOs). PR #2536 has extensive manual description. PR #2555 has minimal 'fix #2498'. Inconsistent depth across PRs.
opportunity_map.adversarial_reason: Template exists but is checklist-only. Varying PR description quality confirmed. Lowest ROI among approved.

recommendations.title: Write CLAUDE.md covering Hydra's package architecture, on-chain validators, HeadLogic state machine, testing strategy, and security-critical paths
recommendations.measurable_outcome: CLAUDE.md exists with >500 words covering: (1) package architecture and module boundaries for all 10 packages, (2) on-chain validator paths in hydra-plutus, (3) HeadLogic state machine overview, (4) testing strategy (property tests, model-based, mutation), (5) build commands (nix develop, cabal test), (6) security-critical paths that require careful review
recommendations.recommended_learning: Review input-output-hk/lace-platform's CLAUDE.md as a reference. Start by writing one paragraph per package describing its role. Then add the security-critical paths section listing hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/*.hs and hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/Crypto.hs. Then add testing strategy referencing Model.hs, Mutation.hs, and the Gen.hs testlib files.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Traces to confirmed placeholder CLAUDE.md. Measurable outcome is specific and verifiable. Scope is clear.

recommendations.title: Create .aiignore listing hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/, hydra-plutus/validators/, hydra-plutus/scripts/, and hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/Crypto.hs
recommendations.measurable_outcome: .aiignore exists at repo root containing at minimum: hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/, hydra-plutus/validators/, hydra-plutus/scripts/, hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/Crypto.hs
recommendations.recommended_learning: Create .aiignore at repo root using .gitignore syntax. List one path per line. Start with the four paths above. Review with the team to add any other sensitive paths (e.g., demo/*.sk private key files). This is a preventive measure before AI tool adoption.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Trivial to create. Specific paths listed. Essential foundation before AI adoption on a financial protocol.

recommendations.title: Use AI to discover untested snapshot liveness invariants in HeadLogicSnapshotSpec.hs based on PR #2560 bug patterns
recommendations.measurable_outcome: At least 3 new property tests added to hydra-node/test/Hydra/HeadLogicSnapshotSpec.hs or hydra-node/test/Hydra/HeadLogicSpec.hs targeting snapshot liveness invariants (version race, deposit coordination, cross-head isolation)
recommendations.recommended_learning: Start with one property: give Claude HeadLogic/State.hs + HeadLogicSnapshotSpec.hs + the PR #2560 description. Ask: 'What snapshot liveness invariants are not covered by existing property tests? Propose 3 property tests targeting the version-race, deposit-mid-snapshot, and cross-head-deposit-leak bug classes.' Review against the formal spec before committing.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Specific bug classes cited. Infrastructure fully in place. Measurable outcome is concrete.

recommendations.title: Use AI to generate mutation test variants for vHead and vDeposit validators targeting post-V2 attack surface
recommendations.measurable_outcome: At least 5 new mutation test cases added to hydra-tx/test/Hydra/Tx/Contract/ targeting vHead validator (Head.hs) and vDeposit validator (Deposit.hs) for mutations not covered by existing CloseInitial.hs and CloseUnused.hs tests
recommendations.recommended_learning: Give Claude hydra-tx/testlib/Test/Hydra/Tx/Mutation.hs + one existing contract test (e.g., CloseInitial.hs) + the validator source (hydra-plutus/src/Hydra/Contract/Head.hs). Ask: 'What mutations of the vHead validator would bypass the existing tests? Generate mutation test cases following the Mutation.hs pattern.' Focus on the deposit/increment flow added in V2.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Specific to post-V2 refactor. Mutation framework exists. Type corrected to kb_gap (no KB readiness criteria).

recommendations.title: Use AI to generate Haddock documentation for underdocumented hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/ transaction modules
recommendations.measurable_outcome: At least 5 modules in hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/ (Close.hs, Contest.hs, Deposit.hs, Increment.hs, Snapshot.hs) have Haddock doc comments on all exported functions and types
recommendations.recommended_learning: Pick hydra-tx/src/Hydra/Tx/Deposit.hs (actively changed in V2). Give Claude the module + modules that import it. Ask: 'Draft Haddock comments for all exported functions explaining purpose, parameters, and relationship to the Hydra protocol.' Review for accuracy against the formal spec and protocol documentation.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Specific modules named. publish-docs.yaml confirms Haddock is configured. Measurable outcome is verifiable.

recommendations.title: Document HeadLogic state machine in CLAUDE.md with AI debugging prompts for snapshot liveness issues
recommendations.measurable_outcome: CLAUDE.md includes a 'Debugging HeadLogic' section with: (1) state machine states and transitions diagram or description, (2) key modules to provide to AI for state tracing (HeadLogic.hs, State.hs, Input.hs, Outcome.hs), (3) at least 2 example AI prompts for debugging snapshot and deposit issues
recommendations.recommended_learning: When debugging a failing test or unexpected state transition: give Claude HeadLogic.hs + State.hs + the failing test output. Ask: 'Trace the state transition step by step. Which precondition is violated?' Use AI for the mechanical state tracing, validate conclusions against the formal spec. Document the pattern in CLAUDE.md so the team can reuse it.
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Combines with rec_hydra_claude_md. Measurable outcome is specific section in CLAUDE.md.

recommendations.title: Use AI to generate structured PR descriptions with 'What changed / Why / How to test' sections for Hydra protocol changes
recommendations.measurable_outcome: 5 consecutive merged PRs include structured descriptions with 'What changed' (bullet points), 'Why' (issue link + context), and 'How to test' sections beyond the existing checklist template
recommendations.recommended_learning: Before submitting a PR, run the diff through Claude and ask: 'Generate a PR description with: What changed (bullet points), Why (context and motivation), How to test (specific steps). Include any breaking changes.' Edit the output — it captures the 'what' well but often misses the 'why.'
recommendations.adversarial_reason: Lowest priority. Template exists but is checklist-only. Incremental improvement.

quadrant.position: High Potential, Low Activity — Untapped opportunity: infrastructure ready, adoption not started

kb_nominations: hs_mutation_test_expansion: AI-assisted mutation test generation for on-chain validators. Discovered in cardano-scaling/hydra — Mutation.hs framework provides pattern for AI to generate new mutation variants targeting specific validators.
